reviews.text:
One of the worst hotels I have ever experienced in Thailand! I've been in backpackers with a better service!! The check-in experience was awful! The front desk was a mess. The room was full of ants - I tried to call the reception - No answer in 4 different calls ( and I waited for at least 2 minutes for each call). The relative-low price and well-known "Marriot" name may seduce you to choose this one but don't be fooled like I was - choose a different hotel!!!

I've been traveling in Thailand for more than eight months - this is by far the worst hospitality and service I have received. Be smart. Choose differently.

My experience was just poor at the Marriott Merlin beach. I reserved for the sandbox programma a beach view room. The one I got smelled moldy on arrival. Well it’s near the beach so I accepted that. But not when I could enjoy everyhting my neighbour did (tv, phone calls). Everything was loud through the connecting doors the room had. 
I spoke to reception. Not for that night another room with beach view was available, but on the night the day after. So that was that. I asked for another normal room then, because they didn’t offer it by them selves, so I could sleep that night. That was granted. But no help moving. 
The next day I got another room, which is just loud at night with constant frogs sound. 
But when during two consecutive days the hotel decided to refurbish the room below me with constant drilling, I really got annoyed and asked again the reception if they could stop this. It took some time but then it did. 
That night I ate at the Thai pantry restaurant and although mushrooms were mentioned on the menu at other dishes it was not mentioned at the one I ordered and one spoon was enough for a full allergic reaction. 
For me this was enough and decided to leave. I told reception I wanted to. But they just didn’t understand, what I wanted, as a sha plus hotel they me gave false information (that I could not leave) , they said reception would pick it up as early in the morning, which they didn’t. I wrote several emails to reception and booking but no response. Until I got to reception again, things could be arranged. The last minute flights were much pricier of course now for me to leave. The manager eventually took care of the situation. 
But I will bet that if you go to the Marriott in half a year the loud connecting doors will be the same, they haven’t changed it (because reception said this was a complaint they heard before). reviews.title: Review
reviews.text:
**Review: Phuket Marriott Merlin Beach**

Upon arrival at the Phuket Marriott Merlin Beach, the reception area initially made a positive impression. However, our experience quickly deteriorated due to several significant issues.

### Room Quality and Location
We were assigned rooms 4220 and 4221, which turned out to be among the worst we've encountered during our stay at five different five-star hotels on this trip. The rooms were outdated and very far from the main amenities. It took us approximately 20 minutes to walk to the pool and an additional 25 minutes to reach the reception. This was particularly challenging as we had young children with us.

### Room Change Request
We requested a room change due to the inconvenience, but were told the hotel was fully booked. However, when other family members arrived later, they were offered room changes with the condition of paying extra charges. This was perplexing since both the hotel's booking system and online availability indicated that there were indeed rooms available. It was very unfortunate to be given such distant rooms, especially with a child.

### Facilities and Amenities
The hotel's facilities are outdated. The pools were just okay and nothing more. The breakfast area was surprisingly small given the number of guests and rooms the hotel accommodates. While the beach itself was nice, it wasn't very clean and not suitable for swimming due to rocky conditions and an abundance of fish.

### Inconveniences
We experienced multiple issues with our room key cards, which repeatedly stopped working, especially if kept near our phones. This required numerous trips back to reception for replacements, adding to our frustration given the long walking distances. The Wi-Fi service was also subpar, which is disappointing for a hotel in this price range.

### Overall Experience
Overall, our stay at the Phuket Marriott Merlin Beach was far from pleasant. The combination of outdated facilities, inconvenient room locations, poor service, and frequent key card issues made our stay stressful and exhausting. For a hotel that is marketed and priced as a five-star establishment, it falls significantly short of expectations. Our kids were very tired from all the walking, and the overall experience was very unfortunate. This hotel is expensive, and it does not live up to its five-star rating.
reviews.room_type: Guest room, 1 King, Pool view

reviews.title: Worst beach in Phuket
reviews.text: The hotel is fine but not exceptional. However, if you are going to Phuket because you want to enjoy a beautiful beach, this is the worst place you can choose. The warning signs posted on the beach say it all: coral shards that can cut you, slippery rocks, dangerous shore breaks, strong currents. For at least 100 meters out, the seabed is full of dead coral boulders, tons of rocks that will hurt your feet and make you slip and fall, and coral shards that can cut you when you do. It is painful to walk on. The seabed is extremely uneven with sharp drops. Unless you are a very good swimmer, this beach is dangerous. As the sign on the beach says "if in doubt, don't go out." Also, when the tide goes, it goes out extremely far and you can't swim there if you wanted to. I've stayed at many beaches on Phuket. Some are spectacular. Most are beautiful in their own ways. This one is the worst by far. I don't know why anyone would build a hotel there. If you want to go to Phuket and sit by a pool, then you will like this place. If you want to swim in a clear ocean on a beautiful beach, avoid this place at all costs.

We had a disappointing stay here.
	•	Check-in: Despite arriving at 11:30 a.m., our rooms were only given to us at 3:30 p.m. after repeated follow-ups with the staff.
	•	Facilities: The hotel is under construction. The pool bar was closed and various areas were blocked off.

	•	Overall standards: Well below expectations compared to other resorts in Phuket.

reviews.title: Not worth it
reviews.text: It's location while being 10 mins out from Patong, is secluded. No where interesting to walk to outside the resort, steep hills surrounding no street food nearby so only option is to eat at expensive hotel restaurants--average meals, triple the price of Patong meals. The rooms are small, the shower temperature ran burning hot to cold with no consistent water temperature every 5 seconds - for the money you pay I expected better. Because of its isolated location and the size of the resort it is very crowded the pools are loud and full of people all day. The beach is beautiful but the reef is nothing to be excited about. Staff themselves don't recommend snorkeling between July and November due to bad visibility, the coral is just grey rock and minimal fish- snorkeling you will find more tattered plastic rubbish than fish.  The resort is beautiful overall and staff very polite but not worth the money
reviews.response:
Dear Lucille,

Thank you for sharing your thoughts with us.  We are disappointed to hear the stay fell below of expectations and sincerely apologize.  Our resort is located in Tri-Trang Beach, a place far from the vibrant nightlife of Patong great for families or travelers who wish to stay in the quiet side of town.  Being a five-star hotel, we do our best to provide value for money in our menus - the costs of maintaining the restaurants and sourcing for local ingredients reflects this.  Since we serve guests from 414 rooms, the pools can be quite crowded but we recommend guests to visit our three pools in the morning when there is less people.  For snorkeling, you also have the option to book tours at other snorkeling sites, where visibility is better.  Regardless, thank you for your feedback.

reviews.positives: The hotel is beachfront, which was lovely. The children’s play area was clean and well maintained. Also, the hotel staff were always smiling and polite.
reviews.negatives:
Unfortunately, our arrival experience was disappointing. After a very long flight, we had to wait a long time for check-in, which was very frustrating.
We had booked the hotel through Booking.com for $5,500, but upon arrival, we were charged $6,000!

The hotel was not very clean, and our room had a bad odor. We informed the staff, and although they tried to help, the smell never fully went away.

We also asked for help booking an elephant tour, mentioning we had two kids with us. We were recommended a tour with the promise that we would see baby elephants. However, there were no baby elephants on the tour. When we mentioned this to the staff member, her response was, “Maybe the baby elephant has grown up!” — not very professional.

The quality of the coffee and breakfast was also disappointing, definitely not first-class.

reviews.title: Disappointing
reviews.text: We are big fans of Marriott International but disappointed with our stay at this particular resort. The resort is perfectly located if you like to be near Patong but not actually in it - just a short 5-10 min tuktuk ride away. The resort has quite a good variety of facilities and eateries but it’s definitely looking and feeling very tired. I was most disappointed with the service - you get sent between multiple different people whenever you ask for something. Even booking a table for dinner is made to feel like a chore and not the ‘well looked after’ feeling you would normally expect from a 5 star International brand hotel.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.negatives:
It was supposed to be our honeymoon trip. As soon as we arrived at the hotel, we heard children crying and shouting everywhere — it was extremely noisy, which is very disappointing for couples without kids. The main issue, however, was the sea. We chose this hotel because it had a private beach, but it turned out to be full of rocks and not suitable for swimming at all — a complete disappointment.

After checking in, we went to see our room and the hotel area, but very quickly realized that we couldn’t stay there. We returned to the reception, paid the cancellation charge, and left the hotel.

We can’t complain about the building or the rooms themselves, but the overall atmosphere and experience were a total failure for us.

reviews.title: Not worth the price
reviews.text: On arrival we were greeted by a lovely girl on the front desk very helpful. On being taken to our room our bags were dumped and we were left to work out where everything was by ourselves not what you would expect from a 5 star hotel. Our room was tiny compared to the hotel we had just come from and the air conditioning left alot to be desired. We had a pool access room that was supposed to be in a quiet area but we had kids that were jumping off our steps yelling and screaming. We had a meal in the Italian restaurant that was really nice but felt smothered by the staff it was really hard to enjoy our meal because we were asked every 5 seconds how everything was.  Drinks are really over priced. Wouldn't stay again didn't think it was worth the money we spent.
reviews.response: Thank you for the review.  We sincerely apologize for falling short of expectations and will look into this for future improvement.  The experience you described is not of Marriott standards and we value your feedback.

reviews.title: Not as expected
reviews.positives: Variety of restaurants.
reviews.negatives:
I recently stayed at your hotel and had a very disappointing experience, particularly with the check-in process and room assignment. Upon arrival, I had to wait approximately 15 minutes to check in, which was far longer than I expected. My reservation was specifically for a room with a pool view, but to my frustration, I was given a different room that did not meet this request.

The situation worsened when I was promised that my room would be changed and my belongings would be transferred to the correct room by 11 AM the following day while I was out. Trusting this assurance, I left for the day. However, when I returned at 3 PM, I discovered that nothing had been done. My room had not been changed, and my belongings were still in the original room.

This level of service is far below what I expected and has significantly marred my experience at your hotel. I hope management takes these issues seriously and implements improvements to prevent future guests from facing similar inconveniences.

reviews.title: Look at the Koreans.^^
reviews.text:
전반적인 숙소 컨디션 나쁘지 않았어요.
저는 풀 엑세스룸이라 수영장이랑 연결되어 있어 편했구요.

다만 발리나 다른 동남아처럼 엄청 친절하다 느낌 못받았고, 성수기에 가격이 천정부수로 올라가요. 성수기에 이가격을 주고 올만하냐는 아닌것 같고 한국인은 성수기 별로 없어요 아마 비싸서겠죠? 중국인 많고 서영인들 많아요, 중국인 절반 서양인 절반 인것 같아요.

수영장 좋았고 바다는 수영하기에 좋은 바다는 아니었어요.

음식은 저랑 입맛에 잘 안맞았고 조식은 비치 조식은 절대 비추예요. ㅋㅋㅋㅋ 경험해보고 싶다면 도전해보세요.

커피는 내부 스타벅스 커피 맛있고 그외 커피는 맛없으니 참조하세요 비싸도 스벅 커피를 꼭 드세요.

reviews.title: Usual high quality from Mariott, plus out of town tranquility
reviews.text:
Great hotel, excellent staff, good facilities though we didn't eat on site once (had booked this way). A good 15min taxi ride (300 bht) into town and very little variety outside the hotel (one Family Mart right outside, one decent restaurant on the hill next door and two massage places either side of the beach).

Quality pools (3 ) and pool facilities, great beach side bar to witness stunning sunsets (happy hour, buy 1 get 1 free from 5-7pm every night). Nice sandy beach but the sea off the private beach is rocky and difficult to navigate for purposes of getting into a position to be able to swim/snorkel. 

Great for families or groups, small or large. Plenty of sunbeds without a need to get down at 5am to save the best ones - we went in a busy period (Lunar New Year).
reviews.response: Thank you for the review.  We're glad to hear you enjoyed the stay and the destination.  In regard to the beach, we offer snorkeling sessions via Sea Bees diving that allows guests to see marine life in the reef.  Thank you again for visiting as we hope to see you back again.

reviews.title: Great Resort for a Quiet Stay, But a Bit Out of the Way
reviews.text: Phuket Marriott Resort and Spa, Merlin Beach offers a peaceful and well-maintained retreat with good cleanliness, friendly staff, and a wide range of facilities and activities to keep guests entertained. The resort provides everything you need, making it ideal for those who prefer to stay in and relax. Its location offers great privacy and a break from the crowds, but it’s not within walking distance to Patong Beach and there isn’t much nearby—just a small convenience store and an elephant feeding and bathing spot. The resort provides a shuttle service to Patong Beach, which takes about 10 minutes depending on traffic, but can stretch to 30–60 minutes during peak hours. On top of that, you may also use ride-hailing apps like Bolt for more flexible transportation. While the overall experience is pleasant, the value for money is on the higher side in Phuket, and the location may not be ideal for first-time visitors looking to explore the town easily.

reviews.title: Great swimming pool, healing, happy first Phuket.
reviews.text:
1. All staff are friendly, and there is nothing that can't be done upon request. It's convenient since they accommodate almost everything.
2. The swimming pool is large and spacious, so it's never boring even after playing for a long time; it was really great. Normally, I didn't pay much attention to the pool when I went to a resort because I spent most of my time in the sea, but at the Marriott Merlin Beach Resort, I realized the importance of the pool!!
3. However, there is nothing to see at the beach in front of the accommodation. There are very few fish, and you can hardly see any corals. I thought they provided fins to rent and didn't bring any, but fins are excluded from rentals. If you don't have them, it's hard to go far, so I recommend bringing them. I'm personally very disappointed about this. If I had brought the fins, I could have explored further and it would have been great...
4. Although the resort provides transportation, it's limited to four times a day, and out of those, only two morning times are free. If you miss the 5 PM shuttle (which is paid, 200B per group), you have to pay more than double on Grab to get to the center of Patong, which is a downside...
5. Everything can be resolved within the resort, and I had a very comfortable and enjoyable time. The breakfast was delicious, making for an unforgettable and happy vacation!

reviews.title: Comfortable with plenty facilities and good food
reviews.text:
Beautiful property - away from all the crowds, yet just a 10-15min drive into the packed patong area.

Stayed for a few nights from Xmas into the new year. Resort was full - however being well spread it didn’t feel crowded.

Rooms are large and clean - with all the usual basic amenities. Had a pool facing room with lovely views. 

Facilities:
Multiple pools - with plenty of sun lounges. The family pool had slides, etc to keep the kids occupied. In addition few games (foosball, ping pong, etc) were available to keep both kids and adults busy.

Gym - was a decent size with few cardio and weight machines to get a good workout in (if you didn’t pick the peak busy times). 

Private beach at your footsteps was clean - a bit rocky but water shoes were available to use.

They also had plenty activities happening through the day - a few kids from our group did attend some of those and enjoyed them.

Restaurants: Few different restaurants onsite - food was good across all of them. The breakfast buffet - was a large spread with western, Asian, and Thai options in plenty. 

We did eat at almost all the restaurants - no complains. The Italian restaurant was good; Thai restaurant we frequented a couple times - was amazing food and always packed; the beach grill and lobby lounge were also perfect spots to eat at any point over the day.

Happy hour on drinks was literally 4-10pm in one place or the other - surprisingly even on New Year’s Eve. 

Staff were always smiling, courteous and helpful - across from the front desk, restaurants, house keeping.

reviews.title: It's perfect for families!
reviews.text:
I stayed in Phuket for 4 nights and 5 days from Japan! It is perfect for families with children, featuring the best hospitality and facilities. There are three pools, each with its own charm: a children's pool with fountains, slides, and a basketball hoop; an adult pool with a pool bar; and a relaxing pool with floating bean bag chairs that can be accessed directly from pool access rooms. All pools have a considerable water volume, and there are no hotels in Japan that have pools of this size. Additionally, although there were people during our stay, we were able to enjoy the pools comfortably without the stress of crowds. Furthermore, the beach right next to the hotel is a completely private beach, where free snorkeling and beach sandals are available for rent. You can see many fish in areas that are about adult waist-deep, and our friends were very satisfied. The hotel has various restaurants of different sizes, allowing both children and adults to enjoy their meals. Although I didn’t use it, there was even a Starbucks for some reason.

We stayed in a pool access room, which allowed us to experience something that cannot be done in Japan: entering the pool directly from our hotel room, making the children very happy.

During breakfast time, the staff perfectly cleaned our room and provided a rich selection of amenities, making our stay very comfortable.

There is a gym available 24 hours a day, as well as a soccer field and an arcade, allowing for a full day of entertainment without getting bored.

If I had to point out a downside, it would be that you cannot get to the bustling Patong Beach without a car. The hotel offers a paid shuttle bus that costs 200 baht for a round trip for one family. Although there are specified times, if you take a taxi instead, it will cost between 300 and 400 baht one way, so using the shuttle bus is better. The shuttle bus has a limit on the number of people, so I recommend making reservations early.
reviews.room_type: Guest room, 1 King, Pool access

reviews.title: 5-10 min to Patong Beach
reviews.text:
The facilities were amazing. Staff always well presented and very kind. As expected, the drinks are far cheaper outside the hotel, but still much cheaper than what I am used to at home. The pools were stunning, and enormous. You never felt crowded, and there were always plenty of lounges and space in the pool. I didn’t eat a lot at the hotel, as I preferred discovering the local markets. The few meals I had couldn’t be faulted. The pizza was surprisingly amazing for a resort in Thailand.
I would highly recommend this resort. I personally would next time like to be walking distance to Patong Beach, it was a bit too far in the heat, so used taxis & tuk-tuks. 250 baht into town and 300 back, so didn’t break the bank.

reviews.text:
Our stay at this property was indeed memorable, although the location wasn't perfect, a 10-minute drive from Patong Beach, it was still decent. This was our second time staying at Marriott Merlin Beach, with our first visit dating back to June 2015. We chose this resort again because we loved their beach back then.

However, upon our second visit, the beach left something to be desired. There were a lot of ants, bugs, and flies, which affected our beach experience negatively. It could certainly be improved.

On the flip side, the hotel facilities were top-notch, and the breakfast was a standout, featuring a delicious spread with a wide range of selections. The cleanliness throughout the property was also impressive.

My friend, who arrived earlier and handled the check-in, encountered a few hiccups with the check-in process and the front desk staff. However, thanks to the efforts of manager Golf, her experience was turned from negative to positive.

We must also commend Pete from the Concierge desk, who was extremely helpful and informative in assisting us with booking excursions. On our last night, OO, a young gentleman at the bell service desk, provided exceptional service for our transfer to Marriott Nai Yang Beach.

Overall, other than an unfortunate experience with the front desk lady Anna and her manager in a red dress, we highly recommend this property, particularly for families or groups looking for a great value and a comfortable stay.

reviews.text: Heavy rain will flood the road just before the hotel and taxi’s will not take you to the door. You have to walk thru the water (knee deep).  Not great after a big night out in Patong.
reviews.response: Thank you for the review.  The months during April - October are heavy rain periods and the floods recede after a short time. Regardless, we appreciate the 9.2 review and hope to see you during the months of December - March as the water would clear up and there will be little chance of heavy rain.

reviews.title: This is by far the BEST family resort to relax and unwind.
reviews.text:
It provides all kinds of the right activities:
- snorkeling nearby, in the private beach.
- 3 beautiful & well-maintained swimming pools
- kids as well as adult activities.
- gym with adequate equipments

We were always greeted with such polite smiles and Sawadhika by all hotel personnel; concierge, receptions, housekeeping, staffs and pool personnel. 

The room and amenities provided are positioned with the guests' best interest at heart. It has a "bar" clothes-hanger in the balcony with several wooden clothes hangers to dry our wet swimsuits.

The room is spacious enough for 2 adults + 2 children. An extra bed is given FREE of charge, unlike 2 other hotels in Phuket and Krabi.

Daily complementary shuttle that runs several times per day to Jungceylon Mall that passes Patong Beach is provided. Family Mart is 5 minutes away from the hotel for us to buy some snacks and light dinners. 

We had a wonderful stay, my kids simply could not get enough and asked to return again next time.

reviews.text:
We stayed at the Phuket Marriot Resort Merlin Beach on our Anniversary holiday and had a memorable stay there. Would certainly recommend it although in late November and early December it was probably not the best time to visit due to the weather. However it was our alternative after our original plan for Bali got canceled due to a volcanic eruption.
The staff is courteous and the resort has adequate facilities besides having a great beach and 3 swimming pools as well as a good spa.
Would certainly consider staying here again in the future.
Thanks Marriot Merlin beach!

There were three clean and large pools, a quiet private beach, and playground equipment for children. It was so fulfilling that it made going out feel like a hassle.

However, there were few supermarkets or convenience stores nearby, and there were steep hills to get to the town, making it quite hot and necessitating a taxi, which can be a bit expensive.

The only downside is that the hotel is far from the city center and requires driving. Overall, we highly recommend this hotel.

reviews.title: It was good, but fell a bit short
reviews.positives: Breakfast selection was okay, service was good. We loved the muay thai night, unfortunately we had an unpleasant experience with the buffet dinner. The pool bar was great, service was really good too. The Thai restaurant served really great dishes. Nice gym. Kids loved the ice cream parlour. Room was nice and clean, very noisy though as ours was right above the live band area. Loved the entertainment areas.
reviews.negatives:
It would have been nicer if all the used towels around the pool areas were cleaned up immediately. It was a bit annoying to have all of these piles of dirty towels everywhere and no one came to collect them, or at least provide a bin to return the dirty towels. It was a bit difficult to find waiters to order beverages as well in the pool lounge area, so we gave up and swam to the pool bar and order from there instead. 
Had a rather unpleasant experience with the buffet dinner during the muay thai night. It was a wonderful event, but my husband and son had mild food poisoning after dinner. 
We ordered fish and chips from their beach grill restaurant and my daughter barely ate anything as the batter was very thick with very little fish inside it was almost like bread. Everything else was good though.

reviews.title: Must Visit
reviews.text:
Stayed as a family plus grandparents. Agoda doesn't list this, but there were connected rooms that we immensely appreciated.
The 3 main pools were well kept and appealed to different styles/moods, and I personally liked the Nahm-Tok Pool (waterfall), as it was mostly shaded.
Food selection was slighlty limited, and one might say the facilities are small, but we overall enjoyed the refined, condensed service the Resort provided.
Sadly we couldn't go snorkeling due to strong waves.

reviews.title: Feel like home luxurious resort hotel
reviews.text:
The Martiot Merlin Beach is a fantastic hotel that exceeded all of my expectations. The location right on the beach is simply unbeatable, and the rooms are clean, modern, and incredibly comfortable. 

But the real standout was the incredible service, especially from staff member Patty. She is always with sunshine-like smile and close follow up from the moment we step into the restaurant "merchant" for breakfast.  Patty - she is a true asset to the hotel.

I would highly recommend the Martiot Merlin Beach. 
Finally, we were surprised when Patty and other staff came to farewell us left us an unforgettable moment 
To be frank, that's the reason we choose marriot group hotels for our familg trips every summer.

We stayed for 4 days 3 nights, there is shuttle service to Patong  for free which was only 15mins away.

The hotel compound was huge with 4 buildings and 3 different pools. There’s about 4 restaurant, offering italian, thai, japan and a beach grill. Aside from the Merchant Kitchen which is for buffet breakfast, lunch and dinner.

We took the Ultimate All Inclusive package which was definitely worth it  as we can to dine any any restaurant for lunch and dinner and order anything from the menu and as for drinks it comes with drinks packages but we also can still order any drinks menu available anywhere.

Chang Thai was amazing for lunch, then do enjoy dinner at Kanpai and D.O.C.G. But we frequently have our breakfast and lunch at Beach Grill as its facing the sea and Tara/Tana the supervisor is super friendly and always welcoming!

Manage to join the Coral 101 class with Pan as well which was very informative and Coffee Drip class with Pond! 

Will surely return again for a longer stay!

reviews.title: Very satisfying accommodation.
reviews.text:
Phuket Marriott Resort and Spa, Merlin Beach

The Phuket Marriott Resort and Spa, Merlin Beach, which we visited for this family trip, was truly beyond our expectations. The trip was even more meaningful because I was with my family, and I think the time spent here will be remembered for a long time.

I am a Marriott membership member, and I felt really good that they took care of everything from check-in. Overall, the staff were very friendly, and I could feel that they were treating us sincerely, not just formally.

Especially in the restaurant, Patt was a tremendous help. They kindly explained the menu and continuously ensured that our family could have a comfortable meal. Despite being busy, Patt always responded with a smile, making meal times even more enjoyable. Thanks to them, every meal felt more delicious and relaxing.

The morning yoga class with Aon was truly wonderful. Aon created a comfortable atmosphere and explained each movement well, allowing beginners to participate without any pressure. The yoga session in nature was pure healing.

Aon also organized the basketball game, which was full of energy and fun. We created one of the best memories of our trip by laughing and running around with my family. It was not just a simple activity; the atmosphere made it even more special.

The room was clean and pleasant, and the entire resort was well-maintained, making my stay very comfortable. Thanks to the quiet and nature-integrated environment, I was able to rest well.

This trip was truly satisfying, and if I come to Phuket again, I would definitely want to visit again. Thanks to Patt and Aon, it became a warmer and more enjoyable trip. Thank you so much! 💙

reviews.title: It was a very comfortable and enjoyable accommodation.
reviews.text:
We stayed for 2 nights and 3 days, with 2 adults and 2 children. We booked all-inclusive.

First of all, the facilities are very clean and great. We moved from another resort after staying there for 4 days, but we didn't see a single mosquito or ant that we had seen at the other resort.

There was also insect repellent (liquid type) available in the accommodation, a very large mesh bag that could easily fit snorkeling equipment for 4 people, outdoor slippers, a scale (which was very useful for weighing luggage), and a Starbucks coffee machine with a tumbler. There were plenty of hangers, so I had enough space to hang swimsuits, underwear, and bathrobes.

If you're only staying at the Marriott, you wouldn't need to bring any small items at all (it would have just been extra baggage). I had booked a city tour after checkout in advance, but I regretted that I should have just checked out and played around nearby before going straight to the airport! That's how well-equipped the facilities are.

There are 3 swimming pools, each with a distinct concept:
(a large pool with floating cushions, the main pool with a cocktail bar at a depth of 1.4m, and a kids' pool with various depths from shallow to 2m, slides, and a playground).
There are so many fish at Merlin Beach right in front of the resort. The water is clear, so you can see everything, and there were so many fish, sea cucumbers, and crabs that we had a lot of fun snorkeling. It was comfortable to snorkel with children because the shallow sea stretches from right in front of the resort to the floating bridge (the water level reached my waist).

It’s child-friendly, and the staff greeted the kids while passing by, always responding kindly with smiles, which was nice. The restaurant staff also greeted us with smiles, saying "Sawasdee krap," which I appreciated. The Japanese restaurant was so delicious that we went there twice... the breakfast was also very tasty... I had pho twice in one day; they served watermelon and mango.

If I go again next time, it will definitely be the Marriott. I want to just stay at the accommodation and do nothing! Haha.

reviews.title: You can spend time very slowly.
reviews.text: At first, the exterior felt a bit dated, but the guest rooms and various facilities have been renovated, making it a wonderful hotel that doesn’t feel old at all. The room was quiet, allowing for a restful sleep. There was a wide variety of food for breakfast, and the staff was friendly, making for a pleasant morning. I arrived at the hotel by renting a car from the airport, and the parking lot had spaces available, which was great. It takes about 5 minutes by car to get to the city of Baton. reviews.title: Excellent service and facilities
reviews.text:
Stayed 6 days. Rented a car.

Hotel amenities are excellent. 
Wide range of food options and  activities for young children are available throughout the day.
Almost everything needed is on hotel grounds. 
One of the activities (foam party by the pool) was an experience the kids loved.
Aside from providing activities to keep the kids occupied, they are also handy should wet weather disrupt your original plans.

The private beach was a big plus! Managed to enjoy snorkelling and observed marine life with the children.  

The family massage by the private beach (on hotel grounds) was nice.  It was enjoyable and calming having a massage with the sound of background waves. It’s appears to be run by independent operators and prices are budget friendly and on par with those in town.

Hotel is far away enough away from the nightlife and buzz of Patong, but close enough if you decide to hit town for entertainment and drinks.
Transport can be easily arranged (if I’m not mistaken, there might be a shuttle service), or you could just grab there.

There are also some good cafes just a stone’s throw away from hotel grounds, for those looking for high ground views overlooking the sea while you have your coffee or meals.

The only gripe for some travellers might be the distance of the closest 7 Eleven store. 

Considering all the pros encountered during my stay. The convenience store distance wasn’t really an issue for myself.

I might have missed some points in this review. For those contemplating this hotel,  research the hotel website.
Prior to my stay, i checked out the hotel’s website mentioning their programs and facilities etc.  Found them to be accurate. 

Have travel Phuket  frequently for the past 12 years.  This hotel is impressive. Will definitely be heading back this hotel soon.

reviews.title: A hotel where I felt the ultimate satisfaction in real life.
reviews.text:
I want to leave a heartfelt review for this truly satisfying hotel. 
Check-in: From the moment I got out of the taxi, the hotel took care of my luggage, as expected from a luxury hotel. During check-in, they offered a welcome drink that included some simple alcohol or beverages. Naturally, considering the hotel's spaciousness, a staff member assisted me in carrying my luggage to the room. 
Room: I booked a pool access room, and its location next to the artificial waterfall made it even more enjoyable. The room remodeling was perfectly done, making it very clean and stylish. There was a bathtub, and plenty of beach supplies were provided. 
Facilities: There are three swimming pools. One is a fun pool with lots of activities, another is a pool with a poolside bar, and the third is accessible from the pool access room. The first two pools are connected. There are various restaurants, each with different happy hour timings, allowing guests to enjoy happy hour at various times.
You can also enjoy snorkeling at the private beach, but the waves can be quite strong, making it challenging. It might be better to wear shoes and go out during low tide to see the fish.
Food: There are two breakfast locations near the beach and close to the pool. The food near the pool is overwhelmingly tasty. After enjoying breakfast at the pool, it's the best to take some fruit and drinks and enjoy them while swimming.
In addition, there are Italian, Thai, and burger restaurants, and the poolside bar was quite tasty as well. The coconut, in particular, was extremely fresh and sweet, and I ordered it every day.

The price was around 350,000 won per night, which I believe is excellent for enjoying a hotel of this quality.

reviews.text:
Phuket is said to be Banyan Tree, but that's not the case. This place is much cheaper than the Banyan Tree I visited, has a lot of things to see, is in a great location, and the private beach and pool are much cleaner with better water quality and well-maintained. If you look at the reviews for Banyan Tree, I believe you will find my beautiful way of speaking even more impressive. 

The only regret and disappointment I have is why I didn't book all 7 nights and 8 days here; I feel guilty about that. I can't believe I booked somewhere else. If it's a honeymoon, you must stay here; Patong Beach is close, there are shuttles, the staff are friendly, and do you know what's really amazing? They serve beer as a welcome drink. Isn't that incredible? 

I hope there isn't a beach that doesn't go to the private beach, and I will conclude my precious review. Phuket is the Marriott Resort? No, Thailand is the Marriott Resort. So, 20,000.
